历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: The minimum admission rankings for Lanzhou University's medical programs in Gansu Province from 2021 to 2025 were 21680, 18712, 15973, 6080, and 15378 respectively. In 2024, due to the first year of the new college entrance examination reform, the ranking system adjustment led to an abnormally low value of 6080, while other years showed a fluctuating downward trend. In terms of enrollment plans, there were 257 students in 2021, 283 in 2022, 239 in 2023, 271 in 2024, and 319 in 2025, showing an overall expansion trend. The 2026 plan is 330 students, an increase of 11 students compared to 2025, with an expansion rate of about 3.45%, exerting some downward pressure on rankings (easier to enter).

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Lanzhou University is a national key university directly under the Ministry of Education, listed among the national 985 Project, 211 Project, and Double First-Class construction universities. It has strong comprehensive strength, and its medical department has a long history, holding a very high reputation and influence in the northwestern region.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: In recent years, the popularity of medical majors has continued to rise, with core majors such as clinical medicine and stomatology being particularly competitive. The scale of medical enrollment within Gansu Province is relatively limited. As the only 985 university's medical department in the province, Lanzhou University has a strong appeal to local candidates, and ranking fluctuations are mainly influenced by adjustments in enrollment plans and the popularity of applications.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Based on the M3 supply-demand elasticity model calculation results and recent ranking trends, the minimum admission ranking for Lanzhou University's medical programs in 2026 is expected to be around 15676, slightly lower than in 2025 (easier to enter), with a fluctuation range of 15440 to 15912. The easing effect brought by expansion and the continuously high popularity of medical applications offset each other, resulting in a slight downward shift in rankings.
